title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Events let JavaScript react to user interactions and browser changes.

Handling events:
• addEventListener(type, handler) — preferred; supports multiple handlers and removal.
• onclick, onfocus, etc. — inline or property-based; only one handler per event.

The Event object is passed to every handler — use it to get target, prevent default behaviour, or stop propagation.

Event bubbling: events fire on the target then bubble up through ancestor elements.
Event delegation: attach ONE listener on a parent to handle events from many child elements — efficient and handles dynamically added elements.

Common events: click, input, change, submit, keydown, keyup, mouseover, resize, DOMContentLoaded, load.
